meta.canonical_chain: phase-fit assignments → stage filter → evals top-N per stage

stages.name: Research / Discovery
stages.gap_note: Bug investigation / code-archaeology absent. POEM Alex pre-greeting scan is the lone deployed system-comprehension instance.

top_winners.name: source-driven-development
top_winners.adoption_fit: strong — Language-agnostic; supports all major stacks via dependency file detection. The principle (don't write from training data — verify) is universally applicable and especially important for rapidly-evolving frameworks.
top_winners.phrase: If you cannot find documentation for a pattern, say so explicitly. Honesty about what you couldn't verify is more valuab…

top_winners.name: context-engineering
top_winners.phrase: Context window size ≠ attention budget. Focused context outperforms large context.

top_winners.name: brain-query
top_winners.phrase: File discovery tool — returns paths for piping into llm_context. Different from focus (orientation) and brain-librarian …

top_winners.name: browse-with-me
top_winners.phrase: David watches. You drive. Pick the right tool, then operate it.

top_winners.name: ralphy
top_winners.adoption_fit: strong — David's primary execution loop. No adoption required; it already runs campaigns. The profile system (Development/Analysis/Content) and Project Heal retroactive backlog builder are particularly mature.
top_winners.phrase: Subagents are defined by what they DON'T receive — task agents get task text + AGENTS.md only.

stages.name: Story Execution Loop
stages.gap_note: Sparse cluster (81). BMAD Amelia is your developer persona — most artifacts are persona-related.

top_winners.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
top_winners.adoption_fit: strong — Language-agnostic debugging discipline; examples use TypeScript/Node but patterns apply universally. No stack lock. Directly composable with incremental-implementation and test-driven-development.
top_winners.phrase: Don't push past a failing test or broken build to work on the next feature. Errors compound.

top_winners.name: frontend-ui-engineering
top_winners.adoption_fit: strong — Language-agnostic principles; examples are React/Tailwind but the concepts (composition over configuration, data/presentation separation, state hierarchy, accessibility rules) transfer broadly. Not stack-locked.
top_winners.phrase: UI that looks like it was built by a design-aware engineer at a top company — not like it was generated by an AI.

top_winners.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
top_winners.phrase: THINGS I DIDN'T TOUCH (intentionally): ... This shows you exercised scope discipline and didn't go on an unsolicited ren…

top_winners.name: incremental-implementation
top_winners.phrase: If you notice something worth improving outside your task scope, note it — don't fix it.

top_winners.name: performance-optimization
top_winners.adoption_fit: strong — Language-agnostic measurement discipline; React/TypeScript examples but the measure→identify→fix→verify→guard workflow and the symptom-tree are universally applicable. Not stack-locked.
top_winners.phrase: Performance work without measurement is guessing — and guessing leads to premature optimization that adds complexity wit…

stages.name: Develop + Unit Test
stages.gap_note: Systematic-debug skill missing entirely. Highest-leverage single gap.

top_winners.phrase: Instead of guessing what's happening at runtime, verify it.

top_winners.name: ce-compound
top_winners.adoption_fit: mid — The skill is tightly coupled to the compound-engineering docs/solutions/ directory structure, YAML schema, and specialized subagents (ce-sessions, ce-performance-oracle, ce-security-sentinel, etc.). The dual-track classification, overlap detection, and discoverability check are directly portable. The full pipeline requires the compound-engineering plugin ecosystem — not a drop-in.
